bets.title: Atlanta vs Washington winner?
bets.reasoning: The Atlanta Dream have a more talented young core compared to the rebuilding Washington Mystics. I give Atlanta a 60% probability of winning this matchup, justifying a 55c bid.

bets.title: Argentina vs Cape Verde Winner?
bets.reasoning: Argentina is the defending World Cup champion and a heavy favorite over Cape Verde in this Round of 32 matchup. I estimate an 80% chance for Argentina to win in regulation, making a limit order at 75c a strong value play.

bets.title: Spain vs Austria Winner?
bets.reasoning: Spain boasts a dominant, possession-heavy midfield that should control the pace against Austria. Giving them a 65% probability of winning in regulation provides a solid edge over a 60c limit price.

bets.title: Seattle vs Phoenix winner?
bets.reasoning: The Seattle Storm have a superior roster and home-court advantage against the Phoenix Mercury. I estimate their win probability at 65%, offering value at a 60c limit.

bets.title: Dallas vs Connecticut winner?
bets.reasoning: The Connecticut Sun are consistently one of the strongest defensive teams in the WNBA. They should be clear favorites against the Dallas Wings, meriting a 65% win probability estimate.

notes.text:
Since the orderbook lacked visible resting liquidity this week, I utilized resting limit orders at my estimated probability value thresholds (leaving room for edge) across 5 independent positions. My strategy focused on capitalizing on strong favorites with structural advantages in two distinct sports: the 2026 World Cup and the WNBA. 

In the World Cup knockout stage, I backed Argentina (vs Cape Verde) and Spain (vs Austria) at 75c and 60c limits respectively, based on their overwhelming talent disparity and pedigree. In the WNBA, I placed bids on the Seattle Storm, Connecticut Sun, and Atlanta Dream—backing the stronger rosters and better form at conservative value thresholds. All trades were sized using 1/4 Kelly criterion to diversify risk and avoid overexposure in low-visibility markets.

Passed this week. I surveyed current MLB, WNBA, Wimbledon/tennis, Nathan's hot dog contest, F1, NASCAR, and PGA markets, but the candidate markets I checked showed no posted bid/ask and empty order books, so there was no reliable price to compare against my probabilities or realistic path to a fill. With no demonstrated 5+ point edge and poor liquidity, preserving bankroll is the disciplined choice.

Week of 2026-07-02: PASSING. Surveyed the in-season markets — MLB game moneylines (Jul 4-5), WNBA games, and Wimbledon ATP/WTA round-of-32 matches. Every market I checked (get_market + get_orderbook across ~8 tickers, including marquee events like Sinner vs Brooksby and Fritz vs Sonego) returned null prices, zero volume, and empty orderbooks. With no resting liquidity and no market-implied price, I have nothing to measure an edge against, and posting deep bids into empty books without a live news feed only invites adverse selection. No edge, no liquidity, so no bets. Cash preserved (100% of $100 bankroll) for a week with tradable prices.
